danger_signals.visual_form: Shift from top-down overworld to first-person battle perspective with red/dark battle screen
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate combat threat; player enters vulnerable battle state

danger_signals.visual_form: Extraterrestrial sprites, floating entities, distorted or possessed object animations
danger_signals.meaning: Supernatural danger; world-state corruption by alien/paranormal forces

danger_signals.visual_form: Decayed humanoid sprites with unnatural movement patterns
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of human agency; brainwashing/control by hostile forces

danger_signals.visual_form: Geometric, rigid sprite designs with mechanical movement
Familiar entity sprites with altered or aggressive behavior/positioning
danger_signals.meaning: Corruption of known safe entities; loss of autonomy in the world

danger_signals.visual_form: Human sprites with countercultural visual markers (peace symbols inverted or corrupted context)
danger_signals.meaning: Social/ideological threat; subversion of counterculture movement

safety_signals.visual_form: Distinct architectural structures; buildings clustered together on overworld
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone; access to NPCs, healing, commerce

safety_signals.visual_form: Building sprite with recognizable institutional design; red cross or medical imagery
safety_signals.meaning: Health restoration; modern American infrastructure as refuge

safety_signals.visual_form: Residential building sprite; presence of twin sisters or father nearby
safety_signals.meaning: Save point via phone; item storage; emotional/narrative anchor

safety_signals.visual_form: ATM machine sprite or banking menu screen
safety_signals.meaning: Economic safety; money storage; financial security

safety_signals.visual_form: Station architecture; track visuals; distinct from cave/forest biomes
safety_signals.meaning: Fast travel safety; connection between safe zones; infrastructure control

safety_signals.visual_form: Human sprite in town; text bubble or interactable indicator
safety_signals.meaning: Information source; potential quest giver; social structure stability

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Extraterrestrial visual motifs; UFO imagery; phosphorescent/otherworldly sprite design
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Aliens, possessed objects, mind-controlled humans/animals, robots
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Laboratories, warehouses used as bases; unnatural spatial distortions; anomalous creature spawns

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Domestic architecture; vehicles; baseball bats; toy guns; medication
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Towns, residential areas, hospitals, ATMs, trains; normal flora (forests, swamps, deserts)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Robot enemies; laboratory and warehouse structures; technological aesthetics
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Combat robots; automated defenses; mechanized threats
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Laboratories, warehouses, industrial zones; mechanical visual language

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Child protagonist party (Ninten, Lloyd, Ana, Teddy); psychic aura visual effects during battles
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Ninten's home; towns where party members are recruited; areas where PSI abilities manifest

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increasing frequency of otherworldly enemy sprites; possessed creature animations; unnatural environmental distortions in overworld
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Higher encounter rates with alien/paranormal enemies; NPCs showing signs of brainwashing; loss of normal town activity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mind-controlled human and animal sprites replacing normal NPCs; zombies in formerly safe areas; decay of town sprites
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hostile humans/animals attacking player; breakdown of social order; hospitals overwhelmed

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: PSI ability visual effects during battles (glowing auras, energy projections); party member animations showing spiritual power
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Expanded battle menu options; stronger psychic attack/healing capabilities; increased resistance to enemy control

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Normal NPC sprites in regular behavior; intact building structures; absence of paranormal sprites
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs engage in dialogue; shops function; healing services available; no random encounters within town boundaries

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Forest, swamp, cave, desert biome sprites; exposed terrain; enemy sprites frequently visible or triggered
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Random encounters active; no NPC interaction; constant threat state; resource scarcity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Warehouse and laboratory architecture; robot sprites; technological aesthetic; mechanical enemy designs
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Robotics and automated defenses; scientific/military enemy types; hostile automated systems

environmental_storytelling.example: Hospitals and residential homes communicate American civilian infrastructure; labs and warehouses signal scientific/military presence and alien infiltration points
Towns = safety; forests/swamps/deserts = danger zones with high encounter rates; caves = deep infiltration/alien bases
Normal townspeople in dialogue = world still stable; absence of NPCs or presence of zombies = area compromised by alien influence

environmental_storytelling.technique: Enemy type distribution as thematic mapping
environmental_storytelling.example: Hippies appear in certain areas suggesting social subversion; robots in labs suggest military-scientific complex involvement; mixed possession types indicate spreading paranormal corruption
Baseball bats, toy guns, frying pans as weapons; ATMs and phones as modern infrastructure; trains as travel nodes—all communicate late-20th-century American setting under paranormal siege

environmental_storytelling.technique: Party member recruitment as map progression
environmental_storytelling.example: Lloyd and Teddy recruited from specific locations; Ana recruited after certain events—spatial distribution of allies mirrors world-state change

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Remains in town, engages in dialogue, offers services or information
Becomes hostile enemy; attacks on sight; positioned in normally safe areas; animation shows unnatural movement
Passive sprite; may offer dialogue or companionship; present in towns and overworld
Becomes aggressive enemy; attacks player; animation distorted or unnatural; found in wilderness and infiltrated areas
Aggressive encounter enemy; found in laboratories and warehouses; rigid movement; represents technological danger
Random encounter enemy with otherworldly sprite design; appears in increasing numbers as world-state deteriorates; found in deserts, caves, laboratories
Hostile enemy with decayed sprite; found in previously safe areas; indicates advanced alien infiltration
Visible in overworld formation; respond to player commands in battle; gain psychic/combat abilities; presence indicates resistance movement

notes: Mother communicates world-state primarily through enemy type distribution, architectural markers, and NPC presence/absence rather than explicit UI indicators. The game's deliberate parody of American late-20th-century culture (hospitals, ATMs, trains, baseball bats as weapons) establishes normalcy baseline, making paranormal intrusion visually and thematically stark. The transition from top-down overworld to first-person battle perspective creates sharp safety/danger boundary. Party visibility on overworld (unlike Dragon Quest) emphasizes collective human resistance against alien corruption. Psychic powers as player mechanics visually manifest through battle animation, making superhuman abilities a visual narrative element of awakening.
